goal: Controlled manual verification of provider-history retention lifecycle before 10pm cron window

steps.error_context: PL/pgSQL function summarize_provider_offer_history_partition(date) line 30 at SQL statement
steps.root_cause: CTE column alias 'snapshot_date' in agg CTE collides with OUT parameter 'snapshot_date' in RETURNS TABLE definition

steps.note: All three dropped partitions contained 0 rows

steps.error_message: canceling statement due to statement timeout
steps.error_context: PL/pgSQL function prune_provider_offers_bounded(integer,integer,integer) line 26 at SQL statement
root_causes: provider_offers is a VIEW over provider_offers_legacy_quarantine (8,291,206 rows)
No created_at index on provider_offers_legacy_quarantine; DELETE subquery performs full sequential scan
statement_timeout = 2min; first 5000-row batch cannot complete within limit

new_defects.title: summarize_provider_offer_history_partition: snapshot_date column reference ambiguous
new_defects.fix: Rename CTE column alias 'snapshot_date' to unambiguous name (e.g. snap_dt) in function body

new_defects.title: prune_provider_offers_bounded: targets view over 8.2M quarantine rows, missing created_at index
new_defects.fix: Add CREATE INDEX ON provider_offers_legacy_quarantine (created_at ASC, id ASC); update function to DELETE from base table directly

gate_recommendation.reason: Two blocking defects prevent the full lifecycle from executing. Drop step passes; summarize and prune both fail. Cron at 03:00 UTC will reproduce both failures.

post_baseline.note: prune did not execute; quarantine row count is unchanged from baseline
